steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: Given \(\frac{2n}{5} = 10\), start by isolating \(n\). Multiply both sides of the equation by 5 to clear the fraction: $$2n = 50$$ Then divide both sides by 2 to solve for \(n\): $$n = 25$$

steps.title: Substitute n into the given expression
steps.content: Now that we have \(n = 25\), substitute this value into the expression \(2n -1\) to find its value: $$2(25) - 1 = 50 - 1$$

steps.title: Calculate result
steps.content: Simplify the expression \(50 - 1\): $$49$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
